title: adhatoda scabra
intro: Adhatoda scabra, also known as Malabar nut or Vasa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and expectorant properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Adhatoda scabra include its ability to reduce cough and fever, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Adhatoda scabra is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
